module_mood_contract: Unsettled competence — 'I wonder if this is right?' Doubt named at Close, not resolved. M3 closer; hands off to M5.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Close (lines 82-90): 'is this actually right?' / 'Hold the doubt. Name it to yourself. Don't fix it here.' / 'Module 5 builds the tools to name what's off systematically. For now, let it stew.' Trace close mood_score 7 — doubt lands, not resolved. Mood contract is Unsettled competence; the draft honours it.
Close framing is not-knowing ('you can't yet say which is which'), not having-done-wrong. Trace mood_note confirms: 'She does name it, then closes the laptop.' No shame register detected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running of downstream teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 90: 'Module 5 builds the tools to name what's off systematically.' The doubt is explicitly deferred to M5; no verification step offered in M3. M4 is not named, preserving the arc gap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Hard-grep returned zero hits for: 'share with', 'sitting next to', 'tell the room', 'compare with', 'discuss with', 'neighbor', 'person next to', 'pause and', 'take 90 seconds', 'take a moment', 'turn to your'. Lines 84-90 (Close prose) are WORK-directed ('Ask Claude to recap... Then ask yourself... Hold the doubt') not cohort-management-directed. ABSENCE of conversation cues = PASS per rule reframe 2026-05-02.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The load-bearing synthesis move is fully fenced (lines 33-45 and 54-66, dual rt-code/rt-cowork). Optional prompt (line 77-79) is correctly marked optional and loosely placed per the carve-out (2026-05-02). Body prose around the Close ('Ask Claude to recap... Hold the doubt') names a follow-up and a mood instruction — these are optional/mood beats, not load-bearing forcing functions. Per carve-out: optional refinement/push-back moves can stay loose in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test — does the student leave the chair while the agent works?
rules_evaluated.evidence: Trace phase_index 1: 'While subagents run she gets up to refill her coffee; comes back to find three short notes.' Subagent parallel execution creates a natural walk-away beat. Student is not pasting each reply back.

Prompt enforces 'Show me before saving' — student reviews before Claude writes. Trace: she approves after reading, rejects the feel-bad action item by not flagging it explicitly but is not forced to accept. Close mood names what's not-yet-known without scripting the student's emotional response.
Both fenced prompt blocks (lines 33-45 and 54-66) contain no [BRACKET] placeholders. Subagent instructions are self-contained. Optional prompt (lines 77-79) is a bare instruction with no brackets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 29: 'Ask Claude to spawn three subagents with different stances, then apply Rumelt's kernel to synthesize their notes back into ./crux.md.' (rt-code). Line 50: equivalent in rt-cowork block. Optional prompt has 'Optional: paste this back to focus the work.' (line 73) — loose but sufficient for an optional beat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 27-47 (rt-code div) and lines 49-68 (rt-cowork div) present paired forks. Both carry the `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` chip. Vocabulary differences correct: rt-code says 'subagents', rt-cowork says 'agents'. Lead-in prose is per-div (acceptable when runtime mechanic changes the ask). Optional prompt (lines 75-79) is outside the fork divs — single block, no fork needed for an optional beat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is an exercise, not a lecture. No lecture placement decision to evaluate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session boundaries explicit — which session, which working directory.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 17-19: 'Stay in the session that wrote ./crux.md, the same one you've used since Name your crux. In the previous exercise this session was relabelled Session 4 (the synthesizer) while three retriever sessions ran alongside it. The retriever sessions can close now; this one stays open as your main session for the synthesis below.' Trace confusion_flag: 'She has two Claude Code windows still open from the previous exercise and has to look at their scrollbacks to figure out which is Session 4.' No explicit instruction to close the retriever sessions before starting; no 'open Claude Code in your Session 4 window' step that names the working directory. For students who stepped away between exercises this is an ambiguity point.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one sentence naming the close action: 'Close any other open sessions first — this one carries the scrollback that the synthesis needs.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing function for the Close recap beat lives in body prose, not a fenced prompt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 84: 'Ask Claude to recap the three retrievals' core claims next to your ## Answer.' This beat anchors the doubt-naming close — the student needs to do this to set up the emotional contrast ('is this actually right?'). It is body prose without a fenced prompt. Trace confusion_flag: 'She is not sure whether she is supposed to paste a new prompt or ask it differently. She improvises.' If the Close recap is load-bearing (it appears to be — the doubt only bites AFTER seeing the retrievals next to the Answer), it needs a fenced prompt. If optional, it should be marked so. Currently treated as mandatory by the student but structurally optional.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either fence the recap ask as a load-bearing Close prompt, or mark it explicitly as 'Optional: if you want to see them side by side before reflecting' — clarify which it is.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea framing (lines 1-13): leads with the capability ('Three voices, three short notes'; 'Multi-agent's second shape'; 'Single artifact carries the full strategic frame'). No failure words in title or framing sections. Frameworks named by discipline (Rumelt's kernel, Roger Martin's test, Sutherland's counterintuitive reframe).

Single bounded arc: spawn three stances → synthesis → doubt-naming close. No sub-numbered phases. The optional prompt is correctly kept optional. Internal arc (do → observe → adjust → observe again) is coherent.

stealing_the_mood: Close mood_score 7 (not 8): Close recap beat (line 84) is body prose without a fenced prompt. Student improvises the ask, lands slightly less confidently than the doubt-naming would ideally produce. The doubt DOES land — 'Hold the doubt' fires correctly — but the structural ambiguity of the recap beat leaves her slightly less settled in the doubt. Facilitator-premium: a trainer can demo the recap ask; in self-study it's improvised. Fixing the recap beat (fencing or marking optional clearly) would take the close to 8.
